expected_discriminator.inconclusive: V2279 still overflows before covering the target window, codeword slide is not acceptable, collection is truncated, or rollback/selftest fails.
expected_discriminator.negative: The widened capture covers the observed execute_start volume without overflow hiding target candidates and still reports zero target hits.
expected_discriminator.positive: At least one widened execute_start function pointer or bounded stack frame intersects the firmware_class/qcacld-HDD target list.

next_runner_contract: Before live work, reread GOAL.md and reconfirm rollback image plus recovery/TWRP availability per AGENTS.md.
After boot, run serial-bridge health checks: a90ctl version, status, and selftest.
Collect helper result plus /cache/native-init-v2279-workqueue-exec-wide.log and /cache/native-init-v2279-tail-perf-regs-codeword.log as private artifacts.
Rollback to the selected baseline, then verify version/status/selftest fail=0 before analysis.
Classify the expanded workqueue execute_start function-pointer window and bounded stack prefix against the same-boot codeword evidence.
Do not run Wi-Fi scan/connect/DHCP/ping; this oracle only needs the boot_wlan route already embedded in the helper.
Publish only redacted metadata, coverage counts, target-hit summaries, and overflow status; keep raw stack/IP/log samples private.
candidates.summary: V2279 built a higher-coverage workqueue execute_start oracle image; the next substantive T1 unit is V2280 rollbackable live validation and same-boot classification of the widened function-pointer window.
why_independent: It does not rerun the V2277 image or repeat the V2278 printed-window boundary.
It raises the workqueue scalar sample map/print limit to 8192, above the 6237 total events seen in V2278.
It keeps stack-IP output bounded to avoid serial-log blow-up while still preserving a stack prefix for context.
